channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Iran says Strait of Hormuz passage to be ensured after US pauses operation
item.description: Navy of Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) says safe transit will be ensured with new procedures in place.

item.title: What is the FDD, the pro-Israel think tank shaping Trump’s Iran policy?
item.description: FDD's talking points on the US-Israel war on Iran have been picked up by the Trump administration.

item.title: How hunger rewires the biology of an entire generation
item.description: The first 1,000 days of a child's life decide how they will grow up. Today, 35 million children spent those days hungry.

item.title: Protests erupt over Israeli settlement sales in New York
item.description: Hundreds protested outside an Israeli real estate event linked to settlement sales in the occupied West Bank.

item.title: Canary Islands refuses to allow MV Hondius with hantavirus to dock
item.description: Three people have died from the disease so far but the WHO says the risk to the public is still 'low'.

item.title: Lessons from the war: A call for strategic reckoning in West Asia
item.description: The Israeli-US war on Iran shattered long-held assumptions about security, deterrence and stability in the region.

item.title: Has the US accepted Iran’s demand to settle Hormuz first, nuclear later?
item.description: US pauses Hormuz escorts after Pakistan-led mediation gains traction, signalling shift towards a limited framework deal.

item.title: Low US hotel bookings paint grim hospitality picture at the World Cup
item.description: Report says several factors, including visa barriers and geopolitical concerns are to be blamed for low booking numbers.

item.title: China calls for a ‘complete’ end to war on Iran
item.description: China calls for a ‘complete’ end to war on Iran

item.title: ‘Operation Epic Fury’ has ended: Is the Iran war over?
item.description: Experts say Trump's pause of 'Project Freedom' could be the 'beginning of the end' for the war on Iran.

item.title: Sudan blames Ethiopia, UAE for recent drone attacks: What we know
item.description: Sudan recalls its ambassador to Addis Ababa as drone attacks shatter sense of calm after years of civil war.

item.title: Saudi Arabia posts $33.5bn budget deficit amid drop in oil sales
item.description: Kingdom announces sharp rise in budget shortfall amid the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Israeli attacks on southern, eastern Lebanon kill at least six people
item.description: Israel issues forced displacement orders for 12 villages in southern Lebanon.

item.title: Colombia tourist jewel plagued by violence
item.description: Armed groups’ extortion and turf wars in Colombia’s Sierra Nevada threaten Indigenous communities and tourism boom.

item.title: FIFA chief Infantino defends World Cup ticket prices
item.description: Eye-watering prices reflect demand to watch the World Cup in the US, Infantino says, amid criticism from fans.

item.title: US petrol prices 50 percent higher than before war on Iran
item.description: The average price of petrol in the United States has reached $4.48 per gallon, according to AAA.

item.title: ‘A stress test’: US-Germany rift widens as Iran war drags on
item.description: German official says US-Europe relationship is being tested as opposition MP says Berlin is 'not neutral'

item.title: Shopping centre fire kills at least eight people in Iran
item.description: Shopping centre fire kills at least eight people in Iran

item.title: Australian women with alleged ISIL ties returning from Syria, minister says
item.description: Australian Federal Police Commissioner Krissy Barrett says some individuals will be arrested and charged.

item.title: Thunder dominate LeBron James, Lakers to win Game 1
item.description: A game-high 27 points by LeBron James were not enough for LA Lakers to upset Oklahoma City in Western Conference semis.

item.title: Syria says it dismantled Hezbollah-linked cell plotting assassinations
item.description: Authorities say the cell was planning 'targeted assassinations against high-level government officials'.

item.title: US must not insult IRGC during the World Cup: Iran’s football chief
item.description: Mehdi Taj says cohosts US must ensure the Iranian military and IRGC are not 'insulted' during the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Top tennis players could boycott French Open over prize money: Sabalenka
item.description: Women's tennis stars say players 'deserve to be paid more' in prize money at Grand Slam tournaments.

item.title: Iran war day 68: Trump talks about progress in talks; Rubio says war ‘over’
item.description: Trump pauses US 'Project Freedom' operation in Strait of Hormuz, citing progress towards Iran agreement.

item.title: Manipur’s ‘unknown’ killers: Three years of India’s bloody ethnic conflict
item.description: Violence in the remote state not only endures, but has mutated and deepened though assailants remain officially unnamed.
